compiled_state.title: Pre-commit test-gate hook times out on large merges despite 'failing open' message — exit 124 blocks commit
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## What happened

The pre-commit-test-gate hook in the husky pre-commit chain times out after 10 seconds when processing large merge commits (31+ staged source files). The hook logs 'WARNING: timed out — failing open (commit allowed)' but exits with code 124, which husky interprets as failure — blocking the commit.

## Reproduction

1. Work on a feature branch with changes across 19+ source files
2. Merge origin/main which adds more changed files (31 total staged)
3. Attempt git commit — test-gate runs record-test-status.sh per-file, exceeds 10s, exits 124

## Expected behavior

Either the 10-second timeout should be increased for merge commits, or exit code 124 should be mapped to exit 0 when the 'failing open' policy applies.

## Observed behavior

Hook prints 'failing open (commit allowed)' then exits 124. Husky treats this as failure, blocking the commit.

## Workaround

Run git commit interactively (the timeout still triggers but the user can retry or the hook can be bypassed).

## Affected component

pre-commit-test-gate hook (DSO plugin)

## Security constraint

Any fix MUST NOT allow agents to bypass the test gate for normal (non-merge) commits. The test gate exists to prevent untested code from being committed. The fix should only apply to the timeout/exit-code behavior for large merge commits where tests have already been verified to pass.

comments.body: Classification: behavioral, Score: 3 (INTERMEDIATE). severity=2 (blocks commits), complexity=1 (multiple subsystems), environment=0 (local).
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body:
Investigation RESULT (INTERMEDIATE):

ROOT CAUSE: Three interacting issues:
1. PRIMARY: Missing fast-path — hook checks test-gate-status AFTER all per-file work (line 462), wasting ~20s when status is already valid
2. SECONDARY: Per-file find traversals in fuzzy_find_associated_tests — 32-48 find traversals for 16 files
3. TERTIARY: Wrapper exit code bug — gtimeout returns 124 regardless of hook's fail-open trap exit 0

All 4 hypotheses confirmed. Recommended fixes:
- Fix 1 (RECOMMENDED): Move status check before per-file work as fast-path
- Fix 3 (MUST FIX): Wrapper sentinel for fail-open intent
- Fix 2 (FOLLOW-UP): Batch fuzzy matching
- Fix 4 (MINOR): Defer auto-pruning past fast-path
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body:
SESSION PROGRESS (2026-03-31):

Fix implemented and committed (35f58c70):
1. Fast-path optimization: pre-commit-test-gate.sh now checks test-gate-status BEFORE per-file fuzzy matching. When status is 'passed' with matching diff hash, exits 0 in ~1s (was 22s).
2. Sentinel mechanism was implemented but reverted after standard-tier code review found it non-functional (child process can't set env vars in parent). Fast-path alone eliminates timeout for normal commits.

Merge to main BLOCKED by:
- .test-index merge conflict (trivial — combine entries from both branches, same resolution 3 times)
- MERGE_HEAD during merge causes classifier tests to see is_merge_commit=true, which makes size_action=none (failing 3 tests from main). This is bug 1849-145d (compute-diff-hash merge-mode).
- Workaround: temporarily remove MERGE_HEAD during record-test-status, but additional test failures from main's new tests prevent 'passed' status.

NEXT SESSION ACTIONS:
1. Resolve .test-index conflict (same pattern each time)
2. Investigate test failures from main's merged changes
3. Use merge-to-main.sh --resume after commit
4. Consider bug 1849-145d fix for compute-diff-hash merge mode
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
